# java_prep Docusaurus Website Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build and deploy a public Docusaurus 3.x documentation site in `notes_web/` that publishes all content from `../java_prep` to GitHub Pages.

**Architecture:** Scaffold a Docusaurus classic preset project, copy 200+ markdown files from `java_prep` preserving folder structure, configure autogenerated sidebar with human-readable category labels, and deploy to GitHub Pages via `npm run deploy`.

**Tech Stack:** Docusaurus 3.x, React 18, Node.js 24, GitHub Pages (`gh-pages` branch)

---

## File Map

| File | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `notes_web/docusaurus.config.js` | Create | Site title, GitHub Pages config, navbar, footer |
| `notes_web/sidebars.js` | Create | Autogenerated sidebar config |
| `notes_web/package.json` | Create | npm dependencies and scripts |
| `notes_web/src/pages/index.js` | Create | Custom landing page with section cards |
| `notes_web/src/css/custom.css` | Create | Minor style overrides |
| `notes_web/docs/**` | Create | All markdown content copied from java_prep |
| `notes_web/docs/*/\_category_.json` | Create | Human-readable labels for each section |
| `notes_web/static/img/` | Create | Favicon/logo placeholder |
| `notes_web/.gitignore` | Create | Ignore node_modules, .docusaurus, build/ |

---

## Task 1: Scaffold Docusaurus project

**Files:**
- Create: `notes_web/package.json`
- Create: `notes_web/docusaurus.config.js`
- Create: `notes_web/sidebars.js`
- Create: `notes_web/.gitignore`
- Create: `notes_web/babel.config.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Run create-docusaurus inside notes_web**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npx create-docusaurus@latest . classic --typescript false --skip-install
```

Expected: Docusaurus scaffolds files into the current directory. You'll see files like `docusaurus.config.js`, `sidebars.js`, `src/`, `static/`, `docs/`, `blog/`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Install dependencies**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npm install
```

Expected: `node_modules/` created, no errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Delete the default docs and blog content**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
rm -rf blog/
rm -rf docs/tutorial-basics docs/tutorial-extras docs/intro.md
```

Expected: The default Docusaurus tutorial pages are removed. The `docs/` folder now only contains `docs/superpowers/` (our spec/plan files).

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify dev server starts**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npm run start -- --no-open
```

Expected: Server starts on `http://localhost:3000`. It may show a "no docs found" warning — that's expected at this stage. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit scaffold**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git init
git add .
git commit -m "feat: scaffold Docusaurus 3.x classic project"
```

---

## Task 2: Configure docusaurus.config.js

**Files:**
- Modify: `notes_web/docusaurus.config.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace docusaurus.config.js with project-specific config**

Replace the entire content of `notes_web/docusaurus.config.js` with:

```js
// @ts-check
const { themes: prismThemes } = require('prism-react-renderer');

/** @type {import('@docusaurus/types').Config} */
const config = {
  title: 'Software Dev Interview Prep',
  tagline: 'A comprehensive guide for senior engineering interviews',
  favicon: 'img/favicon.ico',

  url: 'https://ishansharma191.github.io',
  baseUrl: '/notes_web/',

  organizationName: 'ishansharma191',
  projectName: 'notes_web',

  onBrokenLinks: 'warn',
  onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'warn',

  i18n: {
    defaultLocale: 'en',
    locales: ['en'],
  },

  presets: [
    [
      'classic',
      /** @type {import('@docusaurus/preset-classic').Options} */
      ({
        docs: {
          sidebarPath: require.resolve('./sidebars.js'),
          routeBasePath: 'docs',
        },
        blog: false,
        theme: {
          customCss: require.resolve('./src/css/custom.css'),
        },
      }),
    ],
  ],

  themeConfig:
    /** @type {import('@docusaurus/preset-classic').ThemeConfig} */
    ({
      navbar: {
        title: 'Dev Interview Prep',
        items: [
          {
            type: 'docSidebar',
            sidebarId: 'tutorialSidebar',
            position: 'left',
            label: 'Docs',
          },
          {
            href: 'https://github.com/ishansharma191/notes_web',
            label: 'GitHub',
            position: 'right',
          },
        ],
      },
      footer: {
        style: 'dark',
        copyright: `Copyright © ${new Date().getFullYear()} Ishan Sharma. Built with Docusaurus.`,
      },
      prism: {
        theme: prismThemes.github,
        darkTheme: prismThemes.dracula,
        additionalLanguages: ['java', 'python', 'bash', 'yaml', 'kotlin', 'elixir'],
      },
    }),
};

module.exports = config;
```

> **Note:** Replace `ishansharma191` and `notes_web` with your actual GitHub username and repo name if they differ.

- [ ] **Step 2: Update sidebars.js to use autogenerated**

Replace the entire content of `notes_web/sidebars.js` with:

```js
/** @type {import('@docusaurus/plugin-content-docs').SidebarsConfig} */
const sidebars = {
  tutorialSidebar: [{ type: 'autogenerated', dirName: '.' }],
};

module.exports = sidebars;
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit config**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git add docusaurus.config.js sidebars.js
git commit -m "feat: configure Docusaurus for java_prep site with GitHub Pages"
```

## Task 3: Copy java_prep content into docs/

**Files:**
- Create: `notes_web/docs/00-prerequisite/` (and all subdirectories)
- Create: `notes_web/docs/01-java/` through `notes_web/docs/10-case-studies/`
- Create: `notes_web/docs/interview-prep/`

- [ ] **Step 1: Copy all content folders from java_prep**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web

# Copy each topic folder
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/00-prerequisite docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/01-java docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/02-python docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/03-architecture docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/04-data-integration docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/05-aws docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/06-devops docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/06-leadership docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/07-dsa docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/08-AI docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/09-elixir docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/10-case-studies docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/interview-prep docs/
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/archive docs/

# Copy root-level markdown files as a section
mkdir -p docs/00-root
cp /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/README.md docs/00-root/index.md
cp /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/LEARNING_GUIDE.md docs/00-root/
cp /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/STUDY_TRACKER.md docs/00-root/
```

Expected: All content folders now exist under `docs/`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify file count**

```bash
find /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web/docs -name "*.md" | wc -l
```

Expected: 200+ markdown files. Should be similar to:
```bash
find /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep -name "*.md" | grep -v ".git" | grep -v ".obsidian" | wc -l
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Remove any .obsidian config files that may have been copied**

```bash
find /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web/docs -name "*.json" -not -name "_category_.json" -delete
find /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web/docs -name "*.sh" -delete
```

Expected: Any stray Obsidian workspace JSON or shell scripts are removed.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit content**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git add docs/
git commit -m "feat: copy java_prep content into docs/"
```

cat > docs/05-aws/_category_.json << 'EOF'

EOF

cat > docs/06-devops/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "DevOps", "position": 8 }
EOF

cat > docs/06-leadership/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "Leadership", "position": 9 }
EOF

cat > docs/07-dsa/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "DSA Patterns", "position": 10 }
EOF

cat > docs/08-AI/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "AI & Claude", "position": 11 }
EOF

{ "label": "Elixir", "position": 12 }
EOF

cat > docs/10-case-studies/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "Case Studies", "position": 13 }
EOF

cat > docs/interview-prep/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "Interview Prep", "position": 14 }
EOF

cat > docs/archive/_category_.json << 'EOF'
{ "label": "Archive (Legacy)", "position": 99, "collapsed": true }
EOF

## Task 5: Build a custom landing page

**Files:**
- Modify: `notes_web/src/pages/index.js`
- Modify: `notes_web/src/css/custom.css`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace src/pages/index.js with a project-specific landing page**

Replace the entire content of `notes_web/src/pages/index.js` with:

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import clsx from 'clsx';
import Link from '@docusaurus/Link';
import useDocusaurusContext from '@docusaurus/useDocusaurusContext';
import Layout from '@theme/Layout';
import styles from './index.module.css';

const sections = [
  { label: '📋 Prerequisites', path: '/docs/00-prerequisite', description: 'REST API, gRPC, research papers, coding best practices' },
  { label: '☕ Java', path: '/docs/01-java', description: 'Core Java, JVM internals, Spring ecosystem, OOP, Java 8-21' },
  { label: '🐍 Python', path: '/docs/02-python', description: 'Fundamentals, OOP, async, testing, data science, AI agents' },
  { label: '🏛️ Architecture', path: '/docs/03-architecture', description: 'System design, microservices, Kafka, messaging patterns' },
  { label: '🔗 Data & Integration', path: '/docs/04-data-integration', description: 'Database design, SQL, NoSQL, messaging comparisons' },
  { label: '☁️ AWS', path: '/docs/05-aws', description: 'IAM, VPC networking, S3, Glacier storage' },
  { label: '⚙️ DevOps', path: '/docs/06-devops', description: 'Maven, Gradle, Docker, Kubernetes' },
  { label: '👥 Leadership', path: '/docs/06-leadership', description: 'Technical leadership, team management' },
  { label: '🧮 DSA Patterns', path: '/docs/07-dsa', description: 'Sliding window, two pointers, trees, DP, and 13 more patterns' },
  { label: '🤖 AI & Claude', path: '/docs/08-AI', description: 'Agentic architecture, Claude Code workflows, prompt engineering' },
  { label: '💧 Elixir', path: '/docs/09-elixir', description: 'BEAM/OTP, Phoenix, Ecto, GenServer, supervision trees' },
  { label: '📚 Case Studies', path: '/docs/10-case-studies', description: 'Stripe, Razorpay, Keycloak, Kong, Zerodha integrations' },
  { label: '🎯 Interview Prep', path: '/docs/interview-prep', description: 'Behavioral questions, interview strategies' },
];

function HeroSection() {
  return (
    <header className={clsx('hero hero--primary', styles.heroBanner)}>
      <div className="container">
        <h1 className="hero__title">Software Dev Interview Prep</h1>
        <p className="hero__subtitle">
          A comprehensive knowledge base for senior engineering interviews — Java, System Design, DSA, Python, Elixir, AWS, and more.
        </p>
        <div className={styles.buttons}>
          <Link className="button button--secondary button--lg" to="/docs/00-root">
            📖 Start Reading
          </Link>
          <Link className="button button--outline button--lg" to="/docs/07-dsa" style={{ marginLeft: '1rem' }}>
            🧮 DSA Patterns
          </Link>
        </div>
      </div>
    </header>
  );
}

function SectionCard({ label, path, description }) {
  return (
    <Link to={path} className={styles.card}>
      <h3>{label}</h3>
      <p>{description}</p>
    </Link>
  );
}

export default function Home() {
  const { siteConfig } = useDocusaurusContext();
  return (
    <Layout title={siteConfig.title} description={siteConfig.tagline}>
      <HeroSection />
      <main>
        <section className={styles.sectionGrid}>
          <div className="container">
            <h2 className={styles.sectionHeading}>Browse Topics</h2>
            <div className={styles.grid}>
              {sections.map((s) => (
                <SectionCard key={s.path} {...s} />
              ))}
            </div>
          </div>
        </section>
      </main>
    </Layout>
  );
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create the CSS module for the landing page**

Create `notes_web/src/pages/index.module.css` with:

```css
.heroBanner {
  padding: 4rem 0;
  text-align: center;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.buttons {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-top: 1.5rem;
}

.sectionGrid {
  padding: 3rem 0;
}

.sectionHeading {
  text-align: center;
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}

.grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(260px, 1fr));
  gap: 1.5rem;
}

.card {
  display: block;
  padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
  border: 1px solid var(--ifm-color-emphasis-200);
  border-radius: 8px;
  text-decoration: none !important;
  transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s;
}

.card:hover {
  border-color: var(--ifm-color-primary);
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
}

.card h3 {
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  font-size: 1rem;
}

.card p {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  color: var(--ifm-color-emphasis-700);
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the landing page builds without errors**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npm run build 2>&1 | tail -20
```

Expected: Build completes. You may see warnings about broken links (those are OK at this stage — we'll address them next). Should NOT see React errors or "Cannot find module" errors.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit landing page**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git add src/
git commit -m "feat: add custom landing page with topic section cards"
```

## Task 6: Fix build warnings and verify clean build

**Files:**
- Modify: `notes_web/docusaurus.config.js` (if link warnings are too noisy)

- [ ] **Step 1: Run a full build and capture warnings**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npm run build 2>&1 | grep -E "(error|Error|broken|Broken)" | head -30
```

Expected: Some broken internal link warnings (e.g., links to `STUDY_TRACKER.md` from `README.md`). These are warnings, not errors — the build still succeeds.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify build output exists**

```bash
ls /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web/build/
```

Expected: `build/` directory contains `index.html`, `docs/`, `assets/`, `404.html`, etc.

- [ ] **Step 3: Spot-check a generated page**

```bash
ls /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web/build/docs/07-dsa/
```

Expected: HTML files for DSA pattern pages are present.

- [ ] **Step 4: Run dev server and open in browser to visually verify**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
npm run serve -- --no-open &
echo "Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser"
```

Check:
- Landing page loads with topic cards
- Clicking "DSA Patterns" opens the sidebar with all 16 pattern files
- Clicking into a doc page renders code blocks correctly
- Dark mode toggle works

Stop the server: `kill %1` or Ctrl+C.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit if any fixes were needed**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git add -A
git commit -m "fix: resolve build warnings, verify clean build"
```

## Task 7: Set up GitHub repo and deploy to GitHub Pages

**Files:**
- None (git remote + deploy steps)

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the GitHub repo**

Go to https://github.com/new and create a repo named `notes_web` (public). Do NOT initialize with README.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the remote and push**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git remote add origin https://github.com/ishansharma191/notes_web.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

Expected: All commits pushed to `main` branch.

- [ ] **Step 3: Deploy to GitHub Pages**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
GIT_USER=ishansharma191 npm run deploy
```

Expected: Docusaurus builds the site and force-pushes it to the `gh-pages` branch. Output ends with "Website is live at: https://ishansharma191.github.io/notes_web/".

- [ ] **Step 4: Enable GitHub Pages in repo settings**

1. Go to `https://github.com/ishansharma191/notes_web/settings/pages`
2. Under "Source", select "Deploy from a branch"
3. Branch: `gh-pages`, folder: `/ (root)`
4. Click Save

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify the live site**

Open `https://ishansharma191.github.io/notes_web/` in your browser.

Check:
- Landing page loads
- Sidebar shows all 13 topic sections
- A doc page renders with syntax-highlighted code blocks
- Mobile view (resize browser) looks reasonable

---

## Task 8: Add .gitignore and final cleanup

**Files:**
- Modify: `notes_web/.gitignore`

- [ ] **Step 1: Verify .gitignore excludes build artifacts**

Open `notes_web/.gitignore` and confirm it contains at minimum:

```
# Docusaurus
.docusaurus/
build/
node_modules/
.DS_Store
```

If `create-docusaurus` didn't create this automatically, create it with the above content.

- [ ] **Step 2: Final commit**

```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: verify gitignore excludes build artifacts"
git push origin main
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Done — share the URL**

The site is live at: `https://ishansharma191.github.io/notes_web/`

To update the site after making changes to content or config:
```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
GIT_USER=ishansharma191 npm run deploy
```

To sync fresh content from `java_prep` in the future:
```bash
cd /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/notes_web
rm -rf docs/01-java docs/02-python  # (whichever sections changed)
cp -r /Users/neosoft/IdeaProjects/java_prep/01-java docs/
# ... re-add _category_.json files for any new folders ...
GIT_USER=ishansharma191 npm run deploy
```
